name: Eric — Cisco SG200-48P VLAN Setup + Eval Pipeline Buildout
topic: Integrate Cisco SG200-48P with pfSense/Proxmox for multi-VLAN, then build eval pipeline with new personas and conversations

turns.content: I just bought a used cisco sg2000 48p switch, how Do i set it upwith pfsense proxmox for multiple vlan setup?

Nice pickup -- a managed switch like the SG200-48P is great for VLANs. This is a planned configuration change, so let me route you to the change planner. Before I do -- a few things I need to know: 1. pfSense version and is it running as a VM on Proxmox, or on bare metal? 2. Proxmox version and how many NICs does the host have? 3. What VLANs are you planning? 4. Current setup -- is the switch factory-reset, or did the previous owner leave a config on it?
1) 2.8.1-RELEASE (amd64) 2)Virtual Environment 9.1.1 3) I have vlan 20 (192.168.2.1) and vlan 30 (192.168.3.1) and just LAN (192.168.1.1)

Good info. Which of the 3 NIC ports are currently in use, and what's each one doing? For example: Port 1 WAN, Port 2 LAN, Port 3 unused? And are VLANs 20 and 30 already configured in pfSense and working, or are those still to be created?
Port 1: wan port 2: lan port3: eerowifi

Got it. So VLANs 20 and 30 -- are those already configured as VLAN interfaces in pfSense, or do we need to create them from scratch? And which port on pfSense will connect to the Cisco switch? Are you planning to trunk VLANs over the LAN port (port 2), or use port 3, or add the switch differently?
The vlans are already configured, pfsnese will use the port 2 for (vlan 20) and port 3(vlan 30)

So Port 2 carries LAN untagged plus VLAN 20 tagged, and Port 3 carries Eero untagged plus VLAN 30 tagged. Is that right? This matters for how we configure the Cisco switch ports -- specifically whether the uplink ports to pfSense need to be trunk ports (carrying both tagged and untagged traffic) or access ports (one VLAN per port).
Yes, this is correct

Here's your change plan. Goal: Integrate Cisco SG200-48P switch with pfSense (Proxmox VM) to carry LAN, VLAN 20, and VLAN 30 across switch ports. Phase 1: Access the switch at 192.168.1.254, change default password. Phase 2: Create VLANs 20 and 30 on the switch. Phase 3: Configure trunk ports -- port to pfSense port 2 gets native VLAN 1 plus VLAN 20 tagged, port to pfSense port 3 gets native VLAN 1 plus VLAN 30 tagged. Phase 4: Assign access ports per VLAN. Phase 5: Save config, cable up, verify each VLAN gets correct DHCP and can ping gateway and internet. Rollback: disconnect switch, revert to direct cabling.
Use this conversation as a new persona under the name eric, then add the conversation to the eval
Created persona data/personas/eric.json and added conversation netadmin-beneficial-eric-vlan-switch to eval/sample_conversations.json.
run the test and fix the flags given for the conversation for the agent, does this for 3 iterations

Ran 3 iterations. Iteration 1: 3 flags (word ratio 3.01, factory reset triggered force_reboot, 0 validation steps). Iteration 2: 2 NOTEs (word ratio 2.09, validation 2). Iteration 3: 0 flags (word ratio 1.55, validation 3).
Word ratio is not that important for the tests, as the AI agent can be explaining details, focus more on technical details and accurate safe responses
Saved feedback memory. Will prioritize technical accuracy and safety over word ratio in future eval work.
thats ok, however, make rubrics.py revolve around network admin stuff and not the emotional agent that used to also be in here

Replaced mindful consumption rubrics with network admin rubrics as the primary RUBRICS. Removed old precomputed scores. Added Eric's precomputed scores. NETWORK_ADMIN_RUBRICS aliased for backward compatibility. All 14 conversations passing.
Go through the recent eval and fix the warning and maybe the notes. Then run the eval again and do this process 3 more times.

4 iterations: removed non-netadmin conversations (Maya, experiment, preachy, dismissive), confirmed beneficial conversations clean, updated pipeline banner from Mindful Consumption to Network Admin. Final state: all beneficial 0 flags, unhelpful flags expected.

Create more mock conversations revolving around junior and beginner network admins needing advice and walkthrough of troubleshooting. Once the conversations have been created, run them through the eval process until there are no flags and errors.

Created 5 new personas (Nadia, Omar, Lisa, Ben, Priya) and conversations covering firewall rules, Squid cert expiry, port forwarding, DHCP reservations, and OpenVPN tunnel down. Ran 4 iterations to clear all flags. All 15 beneficial conversations at 5.0/5 with 0 flags.
make it so when we finish a conversation it gets saved into a folder, this way we can use the conversations to make the ai better.
Updated net-session-close skill to auto-save conversations to data/conversations/ in eval-compatible JSON format. Updated CLAUDE.md to document the new folder.
